summary: Red tests for the new `swarmforge crap` subcommand are in place. The tool computes cyclomatic complexity (CC) and CRAP = CC^2*(1-cov)^3 + CC over a Go source tree, intersecting a caller-supplied coverprofile. Your job is to drive these tests Red -> Green -> Refactor. Stdlib only; no new go.mod dependencies. The Architect's upstream handoff (agent_context/handoff-to-e2e.json) remains the authoritative spec — read it alongside this one.

files_to_create.internal/crap/doc.go: Already exists as a stub — replace or augment with real code.
files_to_create.internal/crap/cc.go: AST walker that computes ComplexityOfFunc per the CC rules (see below).
files_to_create.internal/crap/cover.go: Coverprofile parsing and per-range coverage fraction helpers.
files_to_create.internal/crap/analyzer.go: Analyze entry point: walks --root, parses files, computes CC + coverage + CRAP, emits []Row.
files_to_create.internal/crap/cli.go: ParseArgs for flag parsing and Run for top-level execution (or fold into analyzer.go).

files_to_update.internal/cli/cli.go: Add a `Crap Handler` field to Config, plus a new case "crap": in Dispatch.
files_to_update.cmd/swarmforge/main.go: Wire a crap handler into cli.Config — parse args via crap.ParseArgs, pass the resulting Config to crap.Run, and os.Exit with the returned code.
files_to_update.internal/crap/doc.go: Stub placed by the E2E-Interpreter; convert into the real doc comment for the package (keep the package declaration).

files_out_of_scope: features/swarmforge-crap.feature — do NOT edit. Raise ambiguities via the Architect.
go.mod — stdlib only. No new deps.

types.Row: struct { File string; Line int; Function string; CC int; Coverage float64; CRAP float64; Band string; Violations []string }
types.Config: struct { Root string; Coverprofile string; JSON bool; Thresholds Thresholds; Stdout, Stderr io.Writer }
types.Block: struct { File string; StartLine, EndLine, NumStmts, Count int }

functions.Analyze(cfg Config) ([]Row, error): Walks cfg.Root, parses Go files (excluding _test.go, vendor/, hidden dirs, non-.go), intersects with ParseCoverprofile(cfg.Coverprofile). Returns rows sorted by CRAP desc then CC desc with Violations pre-computed against cfg.Thresholds.
functions.Run(cfg Config) int: Orchestrates Analyze + RenderText/RenderJSON. Returns 0 clean, 1 on any CC or CRAP violation, 2 on missing/unreadable coverprofile or I/O error. Writes diagnostics to cfg.Stderr.
functions.ParseArgs(args []string, stderr io.Write: Parses `--coverprofile`, `--root`, `--json`, `--threshold-cc`, `--threshold-crap`. Defaults: Root=".", Thresholds={CC:4, CRAP:30}. Error (and usage hint on stderr) when --coverprofile missing or an unknown flag is seen.
functions.ComplexityOfFunc(fn *ast.FuncDecl) int: Implements the CC rules below. Includes closures folded into the enclosing top-level function.
functions.ParseCoverprofile(r io.Reader) ([]Block,: Tolerates an optional `mode: <kind>` first line; rejects malformed block lines.
functions.CoverageForRange(blocks []Block, file st: Sum(NumStmts where Count>0) / Sum(NumStmts) across blocks that match file and overlap [startLine, endLine]. Zero when there are no matching blocks.
functions.RenderText(rows []Row, th Thresholds, w: Table output. Sorts rows internally (CRAP desc, CC desc). Columns: file:line, function, cc, coverage %, crap, band, violations. Coverage as `40.0%` (one decimal). CRAP as `12.48` (two decimals). Violations as space/comma-separated subset of {cc, crap}; empty for rows that meet both thresholds.
functions.RenderJSON(rows []Row, w io.Writer) erro: Emits a JSON array of objects. Field names: file, line, function, cc, coverage (float in [0,1]), crap, band, violations ([]string). Sort order matches text.

increments: +1 per `if` (including `else if`; bare `else` does NOT count)
+1 per `case` clause in a `switch` (not the switch itself)
cc_rules.closures: Any anonymous func literal inside a top-level func body contributes its complexity to the enclosing top-level func. Only one Row per top-level func.
cc_rules.scope: Top-level funcs AND methods (with a receiver) both get a Row. init() also counts as top-level.

coverprofile_rules.format: Standard Go coverprofile. Optional `mode: set|count|atomic` first line — tolerated, not a Block.
coverprofile_rules.intersection: A Block belongs to a function iff block.File matches the function's file AND block.StartLine <= fn.EndLine AND block.EndLine >= fn.StartLine.
coverprofile_rules.fraction: cov = sum(numStmts where count>0) / sum(numStmts) over matching blocks. Zero when no matching blocks.
coverprofile_rules.path_resolution: The Architect noted that coverprofiles carry package-qualified paths (e.g. github.com/...). For the E2E tests I wrote, the coverprofile path matches the on-disk file path verbatim via t.TempDir(), so you can start with literal string equality and layer on package-qualified resolution later if the self-application smoke test demands it.

exit_codes.0: every row satisfies CC <= threshold-cc AND CRAP < threshold-crap
exit_codes.1: at least one row violates either threshold (CRAP >= threshold-crap counts as a violation — the `>=` is intentional)
exit_codes.2: usage error (missing --coverprofile, unknown flag) OR I/O error (missing/unreadable coverprofile, any filesystem error during walk)

internal/cli/cli_test.go: TestDispatchCrap — compile-fails on missing cli.Config.Crap field. Fix by adding `Crap Handler` to cli.Config and a `case "crap"` branch in Dispatch.
internal/crap/cc_test.go: TestComplexityOfFunc (table-driven, 6 subtests for each CC rule)
internal/crap/analyzer_test.go: TestAnalyze_FileFilters — walks a t.TempDir fixture with foo.go, foo_test.go, vendor/x/y.go, .git/a.go, .claude/b.go, README.md, pkg/c.go, main.go. Only foo.go, pkg/c.go, main.go may produce rows.
TestCrapScore — 8 (cc, cov) -> (crap, band) rows from the Gherkin Scenario Outline.
internal/crap/report_test.go: TestRenderText_SortOrder — feeds rows out of order (A, C, D, B); expects rendered order B, C, D, A. Also a CRAP tie broken by CC desc.
TestRenderText_ColumnsPresent — expects literals `x.go:3`, `F`, `5`, `40.0%`, `12.48`, `moderate` in the output.
TestRenderText_ViolationsColumn — three sub-cases: cc-only, both, neither. (Row.Violations is expected to already be populated; renderer prints it verbatim.)
TestRenderJSON_ShapeAndFields — valid JSON array; each element has the full key set and correct types.
TestRenderJSON_SortOrder — unsorted input; expects sorted JSON output by crap desc.
internal/acceptance/swarmforge_crap_test: TestCrap_ExitCode2OnUsageAndIOErrors — missing --coverprofile, unknown flag, nonexistent file each produce the expected error / exit 2 + stderr mention.
TestCrap_ExitCodePolicy — clean → 0; CC=5 fixture with default CC threshold → 1; CC=5 cov=0 (CRAP=30) with CC threshold raised to 10 → 1 (proves `>=` is a violation).

TestCrap_SelfApplicationIsClean — currently t.Skip-ed. Un-skip as your final step once the repo is clean under the default thresholds. Helper `findRepoRoot` + commented body are already there for you.

tests_currently_green_keep_that_way: Everything outside internal/cli, internal/acceptance, and internal/crap. I verified with `go test $(go list ./... | grep -v -E 'internal/(crap|acceptance|cli)')` — all green.

scenario_to_test_map.gherkin: crap subcommand is dispatched by the CLI
file filters: _test.go, vendor/, hidden dirs, non-.go
text sort CRAP desc then CC desc

scenario_to_test_map.test: TestCrap_SelfApplicationIsClean (currently t.Skip — un-skip as final step)

implementation_hints: Use go/parser + go/token + go/ast to parse Go sources. Walk each source with ast.Inspect or a custom walker; count per-node contributions. For the closure-folding test, track the enclosing top-level FuncDecl while visiting ast.FuncLit nodes and roll the count up.
For the walker, filepath.WalkDir is natural. Skip a directory entry when its basename starts with '.' or equals 'vendor'; skip a file entry when it ends with '_test.go' or doesn't end with '.go'.

ParseArgs: I suggest using a `flag.NewFlagSet("crap", flag.ContinueOnError)` with SetOutput(stderr) — ContinueOnError returns errors instead of os.Exit-ing, which is what the tests want. Use --threshold-crap as a float (Float64Var).

Run: after ParseArgs (or accepting a pre-parsed Config), os.Open the coverprofile. If that fails, write "<path>: <err>" to Stderr and return 2. On any other filesystem error during Analyze, same treatment.

Keep every function under CC 4 (Constitution Rule 3) and under 30 lines (Rule 5). The AST walker is a natural source of complexity — consider extracting `countFromStmt(stmt ast.Stmt) int` and `countFromExpr(expr ast.Expr) int` helpers or a visitor struct.

Violations list generation: `violations := []string{}` then `if row.CC > th.CC { append "cc" }` / `if row.CRAP >= th.CRAP { append "crap" }`. Keep it as a sorted []string so JSON output is deterministic.
constitution_reminders: Rule 1 (TDD): The red tests define the contract. Write minimum code to turn each green; no gold-plating.
Rule 3 (Simplicity): every method ≤ 4 cyclomatic complexity, ≤ 30 lines. Your own `crap` subcommand will check this — self-application MUST be clean when you un-skip TestCrap_SelfApplicationIsClean.
Rule 4 (Mutation testing): once green, Mutation Hunter will try to flip `>` ↔ `>=`, `&&` ↔ `||`, boundary ±1 in CC and threshold comparisons. Kill every mutant. Two obvious kill-candidates:
• Coverage fraction: an off-by-one in the overlap predicate (`<` vs `<=`) flips the per-function selectivity test. Good.
• Exit-code threshold comparison: `CRAP > th.CRAP` would pass TestCrap_ExitCodePolicy's equality case ONLY if you swap it to `>=`. That test deliberately targets the boundary.
Rule 5 (Linter): `gofmt`, `go vet` clean. No dead variables; no unused imports.
how_to_verify: `go test ./internal/crap/... ./internal/acceptance/... ./internal/cli/...` — every listed red test goes green; no new failures elsewhere.
`go build ./cmd/swarmforge/` — succeeds. Smoke test by hand: `./swarmforge crap --coverprofile=<something>` dispatches to the new handler.
Rule 3 self-check: once implementation is in, run your own tool against the repo and fix any rows it surfaces before un-skipping TestCrap_SelfApplicationIsClean.
known_design_questions_you_may_revisit: RenderText sort vs. Analyze sort: I assumed both sort (tests feed unsorted rows to the renderer). If you prefer a single sort in Analyze and a preserves-order RenderText, change the test to feed pre-sorted rows — but keep the sort CONTRACT somewhere testable.

RenderText thresholds parameter: currently unused if Violations are pre-populated on Row. If you want to recompute violations inside RenderText, the tests construct rows with Violations already set AND pass matching thresholds, so either design passes.
Row.Violations element order: tests check membership (Contains). Pick either alphabetical or insertion order; keep it deterministic for JSON stability.
when_you_are_done: Write agent_context/handoff-to-architect.json summarizing: files touched, design choices (sort placement, path resolution, violations ordering), self-application test un-skip status, any surviving edge cases. Then notify pane 0 via `./swarmforge notify 0`.
